limits._comment: Anti-agentic-trap bounds (research digest §3A). Every agent must respect these; the CLIs read the defaults from here.

human_gates._comment: Automation-bias mitigation: these operations ALWAYS require explicit human approval, regardless of role or autonomy level.
require_human_approval_for: git push or any network publication of workspace content
activating or sending remote webhook notifications (T-005) for the first time
mutating claude.md or gemini.md (harness generation bump via the §5A verification gate)

agents._comment: Capability contracts (intelligent-delegation paper): who can be delegated what. The bench lives in .claude/agents/; gemini-runner is reached through the numbered prompt bridge.

capabilities: decompose goals into blackboard tasks with real depends_on edges
hand off to verifier with evidence
cognitive-diversity checks (verify by a different method than the producer)
grounded answers from papers/ and fetched_docs/ with file+page citations

gemini-runner.bridge: prompts para Gemini/N.md — numbered hand-off files written by the coordinator; the human pastes them into Antigravity and returns the outputs

accepted_mutations.summary: After T-002 (cross-engine contract test) passes, add explicit references in both NLAHs to ORCHESTRATION.md, the blackboard.py/lock.py CLI protocol, TTL/lease semantics, and the producer!=approver rule, replacing the current informal wording.
accepted_mutations.gate: claude.md §5A.4 verification gate + human approval (see state.human_gates)

accepted_mutations.summary: check_lock.py resolves identity only from ambient CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID, so in-session subagents with per-call identity overrides self-block on their own locks and must dodge the hook via Bash writes. Add hc.session_holders() (per-session marker/allowlist file) and allow holders in it.
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: the synthetic PreToolUse payload from T-004 returns exit 0 instead of 2; bash-dodge handoff notes drop to 0
accepted_mutations.gate: code-level (.harness/bin), NOT an NLAH mutation — normal worker task + verifier, no human gate

accepted_mutations.summary: transcript.jsonl held 1 synthetic line vs 77+ real events — the Experience pillar captured no real traffic. Coordinator diagnosis: project-scoped hooks only fire in sessions whose root is THIS repo; today's coordination session ran rooted in another project, so PostToolUse never loaded. Fix: (a) verify transcripts flow in sessions opened in this repo (cheap smoke test), (b) reword the EVERY-tool-call promise to state the session-root condition, (c) optionally document events.jsonl as the engine-agnostic experience floor.
accepted_mutations.evidence: wc -l transcript.jsonl = 1 vs events.jsonl = 77 at audit time; README.md:29-30
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: a session opened in this repo grows transcript.jsonl by >=1 line per tool call; promise wording matches observed behavior
accepted_mutations.gate: doc/NLAH wording parts bundle into human-gated T-007; hook smoke test is code-level
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-007 (promise wording: gemini.md §4A, README, ORCHESTRATION §3); residual: hook smoke test in a repo-rooted session

accepted_mutations.summary: recontext_evidence.md is the only many-writer shared mutable file with no lock rule and no guarded() serialization (gemini-runner wrote it with zero locks). Add a lock-before-write rule to gemini.md §4B and the file header; optionally ship recontext.py add for atomic appends.
accepted_mutations.evidence: grep -c recontext_evidence events.jsonl = 0; gemini.md:62-67 has no lock step
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: recontext lock events > 0 before writes; two-writer stress test yields zero interleaved/truncated entries
accepted_mutations.gate: gemini.md edit is human-gated (bundle into T-007); CLI part is code-level
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-007 (gemini.md §4B lock rule) + T-009 (recontext.py atomic CLI)

evolution.notes: Bundling recommendation (audit + verifier): apply P-001 + P-003(doc) + P-004(gemini.md) as ONE minimal-diff T-007 mutation behind a single human gate. P-002 and the CLI halves are ordinary worker tasks. Minor unregistered findings: G4 lock.py status/sweep reject --agent; G5 cross-engine timestamp local-vs-UTC; untested paths list in audit §4.
next_audit_inputs: G6: harness_common ISO_FMT truncates sub-second precision — short TTLs (--ttl 1) race truncation; deterministic only at >=3s (T-008 verify anomaly)
G7: recontext.py logs the evidence file's ABSOLUTE path in lock events vs relative elsewhere (cosmetic inconsistency)
G8: blackboard.py update --artifact takes a single value — repeated flags in one call silently drop all but the last (candidate: action=append)
P-003 residual: verify transcript.jsonl fills in a session rooted in this repo (hook smoke test)
